Condition Monitoring Technologies

A Guide to the Five Major Approaches

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

Overview

Multiple condition monitoring technologies exist, each with strengths and weaknesses. The best approach often combines several technologies for comprehensive asset visibility.

1. Vibration Analysis

Measures mechanical vibration in equipment. Excellent for detecting bearing failures, misalignment, and imbalance. Requires physical sensors installed on equipment.

2. Thermography

Uses thermal imaging to detect heat patterns. Valuable for electrical connections, motor windings, and bearings. Requires line-of-sight to the target.

3. Oil Analysis

Analyzes oil samples for wear debris and contamination. Ideal for gearboxes, bearings, and hydraulic systems. Requires periodic sampling and lab analysis.

4. Electrical Signature Analysis

Analyzes electrical current and voltage of motors. Non-invasive, works with existing infrastructure. Detects mechanical and electrical faults in motor-driven equipment.

5. Acoustic Emission

Monitors high-frequency acoustic signals from equipment. Excellent for early fault detection in bearings and gears. Requires sensitive sensors and careful calibration.

Technology Selection

Choose based on your equipment types, budget, and criticality of assets. Many organizations use multiple technologies for comprehensive coverage of their fleet.
